evals.prompt:
Validate this undergrad resume against Harvard guidelines:

Header: Jane Doe, jane.doe@college.harvard.edu, Cambridge, MA.
Education: Harvard College, A.B. Computer Science, expected May 2026. GPA 3.82. Relevant coursework: CS50, CS61, CS161, CS171.
Experience: Software Engineering Intern, Stripe, Summer 2025.
  - Engineered REST endpoints in Go serving 20K+ req/s; reduced p99 latency 38% via cache tier.
  - Led onboarding redesign with 2 engineers; shipped feature adopted by 15K merchants.
  - Authored 40+ integration tests; raised module coverage from 62% to 94%.
Leadership: President, Harvard CS Club, 2024-2025. Grew membership 40% to 180 members.
Skills: Python, Go, TypeScript, React, Postgres, Docker.
evals.expected_output: JSON with alignment_to_harvard near 95%+, all sections status 'pass', empty issues arrays, and top_3_fixes suggesting only minor polish or none.

evals.prompt:
Validate this resume:

Header: John Smith, john.smith@college.harvard.edu, Cambridge, MA.
Education: Harvard College, A.B. Computer Science, expected May 2027. GPA 3.4. Relevant coursework: CS50.
Experience: Research Assistant, Physics Lab, Fall 2024 - present.
  - I was responsible for helping with data collection.
  - Worked on analysis scripts.
Skills: Programming, Problem Solving, Team Player.
evals.expected_output: JSON flagging education status 'flag' (GPA 3.4 below 3.7 threshold), experience status 'rewrite' (pronouns, 'Responsible for', 'Worked on', no metrics, no action verbs), skills status 'rewrite' (generic soft skills). top_3_fixes should include removing GPA, rewriting both experience bullets with action verbs and metrics, and listing concrete technical skills.

evals.prompt:
Validate this resume:

Header: Maya Patel, maya.patel@college.harvard.edu.
Education: Harvard College, A.B. Applied Math, May 2025. GPA 3.92.
Experience: ML Research Intern, Anthropic, Summer 2024.
  - Implemented RLHF reward-model eval harness in PyTorch; benchmarked 4 candidate models on 18K preference pairs.
  - Authored internal report cited in two downstream design reviews.
  - Presented findings to safety team of 12; design adopted for Claude 3 eval suite.
Leadership: Co-chair, Harvard ML Reading Group. Organized 14 sessions; grew attendance from 8 to 35.
Skills: Python, PyTorch, JAX, C++, SQL, Distributed training, Bayesian inference.
evals.expected_output: JSON with alignment_to_harvard near 95%+, all sections 'pass', and top_3_fixes containing at most minor polish items (e.g. adding location to header).
